title: ADR: CLI-Based Spec Generation Capability
context: Engineering tickets frequently arrive as vague, unstructured text, forcing teams into clarification cycles before work can begin.

The TPDC Engine already has a capability runtime (runCapability), LLM adapter infrastructure (ClaudeCodeAdapter via CLI), and schema validation (Zod). The intake prompt and IntakeArtifact schema are validated and production-ready.

Constraints: the capability is consumed via CLI only; no web UI or API surface is needed. The spec schema is defined (IntakeArtifactSchema). Authentication is not required — this is a local developer tool.
decision: We will register the intake capability as an installable Engine capability that accepts free-form ticket text via the CLI and produces a validated IntakeArtifact JSON, because the capability runtime, adapter, and schema already exist and only need to be formally connected through the Engine's capability installation and invocation path.

inScope: Registration of intake as an installed Engine capability with prompt, input schema, and output schema
CLI invocation path: user runs a command with a ticket JSON file and receives a validated IntakeArtifact
Schema validation of LLM output against IntakeArtifactSchema before returning results
Raw output persistence for debugging and audit
outOfScope: Web UI, API, or any non-CLI delivery surface
Integration with external ticketing systems (Jira, GitHub Issues)
Modification of the intake prompt or IntakeArtifact schema

validationPlan.ac: User can invoke intake capability via CLI with a ticket JSON file and receive a validated IntakeArtifact.
validationPlan.verification: Run the CLI command with a sample ticket JSON; confirm the output is a valid IntakeArtifact that passes schema validation.

validationPlan.ac: LLM output is validated against IntakeArtifactSchema before being returned.
validationPlan.verification: Submit a ticket that produces edge-case output; confirm validation errors are surfaced when schema constraints are violated.

validationPlan.ac: Raw LLM output is persisted alongside the validated artifact for debugging.
validationPlan.verification: After a run, confirm that both the validated JSON and raw output text file exist in the artifacts directory.

risks.risk: Capability installation path is not standardized, causing manual file copying to break on updates
risks.trigger: Prompt or schema is updated in the Gym but the Engine's installed copy is stale
risks.mitigation: Document the export-install workflow; version the capability bundle so staleness is detectable
risks.detection: Engine logs a capability version mismatch warning when a newer bundle exists

risks.risk: LLM output occasionally fails schema validation due to field length or format violations
risks.trigger: Complex or ambiguous inputs cause the LLM to produce verbose fields exceeding schema limits
risks.mitigation: Prompt includes explicit field length constraints and a self-check checklist
risks.detection: Validation error rate tracked in test suite reports; any rate above 10% triggers prompt review

risks.risk: CLI timeout causes lost runs on complex inputs
risks.trigger: LLM takes longer than the configured timeout to produce output for large or ambiguous inputs
risks.mitigation: Set a generous default timeout (300s); allow per-run override via CLI flag or env var
risks.detection: Timeout errors in run metadata; test suite tracks timeout frequency

alternatives.name: Expose intake as an HTTP API endpoint instead of CLI
alternatives.reasonRejected: No API surface is needed — this is a local developer tool. Adding an HTTP layer increases complexity without user benefit.

alternatives.name: Skip formal capability registration and hardcode intake in the CLI
alternatives.reasonRejected: Hardcoding prevents the Engine from supporting additional capabilities through the same installation path, undermining the plugin-first architecture.
